Name: Rent / Mortgage
Notes: Your rent or mortgage payment. This is usually your biggest expense.

Name: Electric
Notes: Varies by season. Use your average or last month's bill.

Name: Water / Sewer
Notes: Some apartments include this in rent -- if so, skip it.

Name: Internet
Notes: Home internet. Don't forget to include the actual price, not the promo rate if it expired.

Name: Cell Phone
Notes: Your share if you're on a family plan, or the full bill if you have your own.

Name: Renters / Home Insurance
Notes: Renters insurance is cheap and worth it. Homeowners insurance may be rolled into your mortgage.

Name: Streaming Services
Notes: Netflix, Spotify, YouTube, Disney+, etc. Add them all up -- they sneak up on you.

Name: Car Payment
Notes: If you have a car loan or lease. Skip if your car is paid off.

Name: Car Insurance
Notes: Monthly cost. If you pay every 6 months, divide by 6.

Name: Gas / Fuel
Notes: Estimate based on your commute. Track it for a month if you're not sure.

Name: Student Loans
Notes: Monthly minimum payment. Put 0 if you don't have any.

Name: Credit Card Minimum
Notes: The minimum payment on any credit card balance. Ideally you're paying more than the minimum.

Name: Gym Membership
Notes: Planet Fitness, YMCA, whatever. Cancel if you haven't gone in 3 months.

Name: Other
Notes: Gaming subscriptions, app subscriptions, Patreon, anything recurring.
